Challenge 6: Better-Quasi-Ordering of Finite Graphs under Minor Relation

Definition 1 (Cumulative Hierarchy over Q).

Given a set Q, we denote by 𝒫∗ the set of nonempty subsets of Q. We define for each ordinal α a set Vα∗⁢(Q) recursively as follows:

  • •

    V0∗⁢(Q):=Q.

  • •

    Vβ+1∗:=𝒫∗⁢𝒱β∗

  • •

    Vλ∗⁢(Q):=⋃˙α<λ⁢Vα∗⁢(Q) for any limit ordinal λ.

The class given by the union of all these sets is denoted V∗⁢(Q).

Definition 2 (α-well-quasiordering).

Given a quasi-order (Q,≤), we define a relation ≤∗ on V∗⁢(Q) in terms of a 2-player game between Player I and Player II. A position in this game is a pair (X,Y) with X and Y in V∗⁢(Q). If X and Y are both in Q then this position is a win for Player II if X≤Y and a win for Player I otherwise. If not, then Player I names a set X′, which must be X itself if X∈Q and otherwise must be an element of X. Then Player II also names a set Y′, which must be Y itself if Y∈Q and otherwise must be an element of Y. After this, the new position is (X′,Y′). We define X≤∗Y to mean that Player II has a winning strategy in this game with starting position (X,Y).

We say that (Q,≤) is α-well-quasiordered if the restriction of this game to Vα∗⁢(Q) is well-quasiordered.

Note that (Q,≤) is better-quasi-ordered if and only if it is ω1-well-quasi-ordered.

Conjecture 1.1.

Let r be an ordinal number. If r is even, of the form α⁢.2, then finite planar graphs are α-well-quasi-ordered with respect to the minor relation. If r is odd, of the form α⁢.2+1, then finite graphs are α-well-quasi-ordered.

References
